description:
lib.rs:252-264 — In put():

    let bytes = value.into_bytes();     // Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>
    ...
    let data = MaxNvBuffer::try_from(bytes.to_vec())  // plain Vec<u8> copy

bytes.to_vec() creates a non-Zeroizing copy of the secret material. This copy is passed into MaxNvBuffer and eventually dropped normally — the secret bytes remain in the process's memory until the allocator reuses that page.

This is not a hot-path performance problem — it is a single allocation on a write path. But for a secrets management crate, leaving an unzeroized copy of secret material in memory is the more important concern.

Fix: check whether MaxNvBuffer::try_from can accept the inner Vec directly (via Deref or AsRef), or whether tss-esapi provides a way to avoid the copy. If not, at minimum document the limitation.
title: put() leaks unzeroized copy of secret bytes
